Hero.eyebrow: Available for senior / lead engineering roles
Hero.roleSub: Product engineering · Platform systems · Delivery leadership
Hero.bio: I help product teams turn complex systems into reliable releases. Over 8 years, I have shipped React/Next.js products, backend services, Kubernetes-based platforms, secure financial integrations, and engineering operating models. Currently leading an 11-person delivery team building multi-tenant rollout systems and enterprise integrations.

proof.scale: 80M+ users reached through Zalo and platform products
proof.team: 11-person delivery team led across development and QC
proof.platform: 30+ tenant rollout platform with secure integrations

stats.yearsShippingCaption: Production code, day in day out

Sections.aboutTitle: The profile recruiters can scan in one minute
Sections.experienceTitle: Impact across product, platform, and leadership

Summary.intro1: Senior full-stack engineer and technical lead with a Bachelor of Information Technology (minor in Software Engineering, GPA 3.36) and 8+ years shipping production software across consumer-scale, CDP, fintech, education, and real-estate domains.
Summary.intro2: My strongest value sits between product delivery and platform reliability: translating ambiguous business goals into front-end systems, backend services, rollout workflows, observability, CI/CD, and stable production operations.

endToEnd.title: Product-to-platform ownership
endToEnd.body: Take ambiguous product needs through UI, API, release, observability, and incident follow-up.

multiTenant.title: Progressive delivery systems
multiTenant.body: Feature flags, segment rollouts, JSON-config values, stateless evaluation, and safe production releases.

leadership.title: Calm technical leadership
leadership.body: Lead developers and QC through ownership design, hiring, RFCs, runbooks, rollout playbooks, and 1:1 coaching.

fintech.title: Secure integration work
fintech.body: RSA-4096 handshake, AES-256 payloads, mTLS, certificate pinning, idempotent contracts, and retry queues.

mfe.title: Scalable front-end architecture
mfe.body: React/Next.js, design systems, Angular-hosted Micro-Frontends, and lightweight SDKs under tight performance budgets.

ai.title: AI-assisted engineering ops
ai.body: Built internal agents for code review, deployment assistance, service quotas, API health, and release analysis.

skills.backend: Back-end: Node.js, Java Spring, and .NET Core for REST/GraphQL services, auth, encryption, backward-compatible contracts, and third-party enterprise integrations.
skills.frontend: Front-end: React, Next.js, TypeScript, React Native, Angular-hosted Micro-Frontends, Storybook design systems, state management, and performance-sensitive UI architecture.
skills.databases: Databases: MySQL schema design, indexing, query optimization, transaction boundaries, migration planning, and consistency trade-offs in higher-throughput systems.
skills.infra: Infrastructure & Platform: Docker, Kubernetes, AKS, EKS, FKE, K3S, ArgoCD, CI/CD, environment configuration, domain/SSL operations, and production recovery work.
skills.architecture: Architecture & Delivery: Multi-tenant architecture, feature-flag-driven rollout, Micro-Frontend composition, progressive delivery, observability, RFCs, and runbooks.
skills.testing: Testing & Quality: Jest, Cucumber, Puppeteer, Testing Library, BDD/E2E coverage, release checklists, rollback readiness, and regression prevention.
skills.libraries: Libraries & Platforms: Firebase, Strapi, Directus, React Flow, Webpack, Redux, React Query, G2plot, PostHog, Electron, and mobile app tooling.
skills.other: Ways of working: Hiring, onboarding, coaching, documentation-first delivery, cross-team alignment, stakeholder communication, and practical AI-assisted engineering.

ndsvn.summary: Stepped into technical ownership after a CTO transition, leading product delivery, architecture decisions, rollout systems, enterprise integrations, and cross-team execution for a delivery team of 8 developers and 3 QC engineers.
contributions: Engineered a .NET Core multi-tenant feature-flag platform for 30+ tenants and ~40k users, with priority rules, tenant/segment/env rollouts, JSON-config values, and stateless runtime evaluation.
Built secure Gtel & Napas integrations using RSA-4096 handshakes, AES-256 payload encryption, mTLS, certificate pinning, idempotent contracts, retry queues, and ACK-driven monitoring.
Implemented Micro-Frontend architecture with an Angular host and React modules, enabling independent deploy cycles, feature isolation, and a smaller regression surface.
Stabilized inherited infrastructure across ArgoCD, CI/CD, domains/SSL, and AKS/FKE/K3S/EKS, turning recurring unknowns into diagnosable and recoverable systems.
Introduced PostHog-based observability for rollout impact, feature adoption, anomaly detection, and regression tracing across release cycles.
Built AI-assisted engineering agents for code review, deployment support, service-quota checks, API health monitoring, and release analysis.
Rebuilt engineering operations: org chart, competency matrix, ownership boundaries, branch strategy, release tagging, RFCs, runbooks, onboarding, and structured interviews.

zalo.summary1: Core engineer on cross-platform Zalo PC (Web, macOS, Windows), serving 15M+ MAU, with ownership across product features, tracking, design-system work, and release stability.
zalo.summary2: Also contributed to Zalo Android, improving feature stability and performance for a mobile product used by nearly 80 million users.
contributions: Delivered 10+ product features, including dark mode, download flows, Kiki assistant surfaces, logging, and behavior tracking.
Built a promotion flow with integrated tracking and monitoring, supporting activation across 15M+ MAU.
Led Storybook-based design-system work that improved UI consistency and reuse for 50+ developers.
Resolved support and rollout issues for 50+ client users while keeping feature quality and release confidence high.
Maintained 3+ Android features, focusing on stability, performance, and user experience at near-80M-user scale.

primedata.summary: Built CDxP, a customer data platform for identifying, unifying, and activating customer profiles from heterogeneous schemas across disconnected systems.
contributions: Led a front-end team of 4, delivering 10+ projects across the CDxP app, Magento/WordPress demos, JS SDKs, and mobile SDKs.
Shipped advanced product surfaces including 360 customer profiles, campaign builders, analytics, segmentation, and partner-facing demos.
Developed a lightweight JS SDK with full tracking/engagement capabilities under 200KB and <150ms load time.
Built reusable UI utilities, RBAC hooks, data-visualization flows, and testing coverage that improved delivery speed and maintainability.

splus.summary: An out-sourcing company. Most projects revolve around mobile and web apps.
contributions: With expertise in the end-to-end software production process, I led a small team, mastering the phases from requirements gathering to project security, laying the groundwork for future leadership roles.
Engaging in 8+ diverse projects across education, food and drink, banking, and e-commerce domains, I gained invaluable insights into both web and mobile application development.
Helped secure and deliver key projects such as Savyu and Bank Tool, generating over $20,000 in revenue and earning Best Rookie of the Year recognition.

propman.summary: A real estate-domain startup. I was one of the founding engineers.
contributions: Initiated involvement in primary web app development as a Front-end Developer. Obtained foundational web development skills, with a specialization in React JS and related technologies. Collaborated on responsive design projects, prioritizing enhancements for user experience.

accomplishments: Owned product engineering from UI/UX to analytics, reaching 10,000+ visits and 3,000+ signups within two months.
Improved acquisition with technical SEO, PWA installability, and a mobile-first app release path.
Kept infrastructure lean on a single Cloud Run instance (1 CPU, 256 MiB) while adding Google Ads and Stripe monetization.
Led front-end architecture for CDxP, delivering profiles, campaigns, analytics, segmentation, and partner-facing interfaces.
Built RBAC hooks, nested routing, shared utilities, and modular UI patterns that improved maintainability across products.
Established BDD/E2E coverage with 100+ scenarios and 1,000+ test cases, helping reduce regression risk and production defects.
Built 5+ vanilla JS modules for tracking, web popups, push notifications, WebSocket messaging, and embedded web components.
Used web workers, service workers, and multi-tab coordination to keep background work resilient under real browser constraints.
Delivered a lightweight integration footprint under 200KB and <150ms load time before deeper bundle optimizations.
Designed and taught a front-end project course for 12 students, guiding teams from requirements to working React/Next.js projects.
Provided review, mentoring, and practical delivery habits aligned with real industry workflows.
Built a React Native house-management app with Realm DB, reusable components, smooth interactions, and native-module integration.
Handled debugging and performance profiling across mobile constraints while adapting quickly to new implementation requirements.
Built a full-stack banking tool to ingest and process 20+ Excel files, each with 20,000+ records.
Designed the workflow for progressive transaction calculations and reliable operational reporting.
Delivered merchant and consumer mobile app features with React Native, plus admin web flows for user management.
Worked across mobile, web, and backend integration to ship a coherent marketplace experience.

CTA.title: Need someone who can ship and stabilize?
CTA.body: I am strongest where product delivery, system reliability, and team clarity need to move together. Send the problem, not just the job description.

home.title: Nguyen Le Phong — Senior Software Engineer & Technical Lead
home.description: Senior full-stack engineer and technical lead with 8+ years shipping product, platform, Micro-Frontend, Kubernetes, secure fintech integration, and rollout systems.

studio.intro: A public shelf for learning notes, machine setup, AI tooling, and the small checklists that make a new engineering laptop feel ready.

gallery.title: Signals behind the work
gallery.sub: A small collection of what I have learned, built, earned, and kept practicing. If the resume shows the roles, this page shows the working rhythm behind them.

hero.title: Small signals that say more than a few resume lines
hero.lead: I keep certificates, recognitions, project snapshots, and a few ordinary moments here so readers can see how I learn, work with teams, and keep delivery steady over time.
hero.scanNote: For recruiters scanning quickly: start with the story, then open each proof group when you need details.

learning.title: A habit of learning and updating
learning.body: The certificates are not isolated badges; they show a habit of learning to handle work with more care.

story.title: Not a photo wall, but a trace of steady progress
story.body: Each group answers a small question: what I learned, where the work was trusted, what I built, and how I train endurance outside formal project work.

categoryNotes.all: Read the full path from learning to delivery in one flow.

categoryNotes.awards: Recognition for ownership, steadiness, and responsibility.

certificates.body: This section shows how I keep strengthening the base: learning to understand systems better, and to be more careful with compliance, security, and product quality.
certificates.signal: Steady learning helps the work become more careful over time.

awards.body: These recognitions are not endpoints. They are small markers from periods where I picked things up quickly, carried responsibility, and stayed with longer challenges.
awards.signal: Real team environments show whether consistency can be trusted.

projects.body: A few slices from products, demos, and coursework. The point is not only the screenshot, but the process of turning an idea into something runnable, explainable, and improvable.
projects.signal: Ideas matter more when they become something people can run, explain, and improve.

activities.body: Not every career signal lives inside a ticket or release. Some habits, work corners, and personal rhythms help me keep enough energy to work sustainably.
activities.signal: Personal discipline helps keep the pace steady for a longer road with the team.

blog.relatedIntro: A few nearby pieces to keep following the same thread with more context.

hero.title: I build product systems that can be shipped, observed, and recovered.
hero.lead: My work now sits between product engineering and platform reliability: front-end surfaces, backend services, secure integrations, rollout systems, Kubernetes infrastructure, and the operating model that lets a team release with less guesswork.

metrics.label: people in the delivery team I lead

systems.title: The layer I operate in now
systems.intro: I still care about polished user interfaces, but my current value is broader: connecting product decisions with backend contracts, infrastructure paths, release control, and production diagnosis.

items.title: Backend services & secure integration
items.body: I work with .NET Core, Node.js, and Java Spring services, focusing on REST/GraphQL contracts, auth, encryption, backward compatibility, retry behavior, idempotency, and partner-system instability.

items.title: Platform, infra & traffic path
items.body: I have been involved in the production path around Docker, Kubernetes, AKS/FKE/K3S/EKS, ArgoCD, CI/CD, domains/SSL, ingress and load-balancer routing, and recovery work when the system is already under pressure.

items.title: Progressive delivery
items.body: I engineered a multi-tenant feature-flag platform with priority rules, tenant/segment/env rollout, JSON-config values, and stateless runtime evaluation so releases can move gradually instead of all at once.

items.title: Observability & operating clarity
items.body: I build the feedback loops around releases: PostHog, metrics pipelines, API health checks, anomaly review, rollback readiness, RFCs, runbooks, and release criteria that make incidents easier to understand.

stack.title: The engineering details I pay attention to
stack.intro: The systems I prefer are not only feature-complete. They have clear contracts, known failure modes, and enough operational signs for the team to find the problem before users feel it.

groups.title: Backend techniques
items: Backward-compatible API contracts for mobile, web, and enterprise clients.
RSA-4096 handshake, AES-256 payload encryption, mTLS, certificate pinning, and ACK-driven monitoring for financial integrations.
Retry queues and idempotent APIs for partners that are slow, unstable, or asynchronous.
Stateless runtime evaluation for rollout rules, JSON config, tenant and segment targeting.

groups.title: Infrastructure techniques
items: Kubernetes cluster operations across AKS, FKE, K3S, and EKS environments.
Ingress, load balancer, domain and SSL paths treated as part of the product delivery flow, not as a black box.
ArgoCD and CI/CD pipelines for repeatable releases, environment control, and recovery.
Production diagnosis through logs, metrics, health checks, and documented recovery steps.

groups.title: Release architecture
items: Micro-Frontend composition with Angular host and React modules to reduce deployment coupling.
Feature-flag rollout by tenant, segment, environment, and percentage.
Observability workflows for feature adoption, regression tracing, and anomaly review.
Rollback criteria and release checklists so delivery stays deliberate under pressure.

groups.title: Team systems
items: Ownership boundaries, org chart, competency matrix, and workload routing after leadership transition.
RFCs, runbooks, technical specs, and troubleshooting guides that reduce hidden knowledge.
Hiring, onboarding, 1:1 coaching, and review loops for developers and QC engineers.
Branch strategy, commit convention, release tagging, and integration rules that keep delivery predictable.

principles.title: Principles I bring into production systems
principles.intro: These are practical habits, not slogans. They come from seeing systems fail in ordinary ways and learning how to make the next release easier to reason about.

items.title: Trace before rewriting
items.body: When a system behaves strangely, I first look for the path: request flow, data contract, logs, metrics, release history, and infrastructure changes.

items.title: Contracts before code volume
items.body: A good backend service is easier to operate when its API, retries, idempotency, security boundary, and compatibility rules are clear.

items.title: Rollout before big-bang release
items.body: Feature flags, tenant targeting, staged deployment, and rollback criteria keep product movement steady without making every release a gamble.

items.title: Documentation should reduce interruption
items.body: Specs, runbooks, and checklists are useful when they let another engineer diagnose, recover, or continue the work without waiting for one person.

closing.label: About closing note
closing.body: I am most useful in teams where product ambition, backend complexity, and production reliability meet. That is where careful engineering turns unclear systems into something the team can ship, observe, and improve.
